title: Cell DIVE™ Platform | Slide Clearing and Antigen Retrieval
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"rnv5","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to manually deparaffinize and rehydrate slides for the Cell DIVE™ Platform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aacjm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"67s3v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

{"blocks":[{"key":"4h96","text":"Definitions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgoc3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t668","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a2jq4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"um7f","text":"Descriptions ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ibmt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3adsh","text":"Paraffin embedded formalin fixed slides have to be cleared first to (1) remove the wax out with xylene and (2) rehydrate the tissue back into buffer with a series of ethanol, etc. steps. After clearing, formalin bridges have to be uncrossed by incubating the tissue slides in a pressure cooker with antigen retrieval solutions. This document covers the slide clearing and antigen retrieval process using manual protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eh5ls","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eprtj","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a85sr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7jf5i","text":" before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"sh07","text":"Please take particular note of the following instructions regarding critical steps: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":84}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9jd14","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62iij","text":"It is essential to read the complete instruction booklet before starting work.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eggo5","text":"These instructions have only been validated on formalin-fixed paraffin embedded tissue sections.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a784p","text":"Unless noted, it is essential to allow reagents discussed to reach room temperature prior to use. We therefore recommend that this product is handled only by those persons who have been trained in laboratory techniques and that it is used in accordance with the principles of good laboratory practice. Wear suitable protective clothing such as laboratory coats, safety glasses, and gloves. Care should be taken to avoid contact with skin or eyes. In the case of contact with skin or eyes wash immediately with water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Shake stock unmasking solution vigorously prior to pipetting. Mix water and stock unmasking solution thoroughly in beaker. Make fresh prior to each antigen retrieval step. The pH should be 6.0"],["Tris Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS2)","If you’re using white coplin jars (50 mLs) -
 5 mL 10x Tris AR stock
45 mL ddH20
-----------------------
50 mL Total Volume

If you’re using green staining jars (hold ~250 mL)
 25 mL 10x Tris AR stock
 225 mL ddH20
-----------------------
 250 mL Total Volume

Mix water and stock Tris AR thoroughly in beaker.
Make fresh prior to each antigen retrieval step.
The pH should be 8.8-9.0"],["Slide Blocking Solution (10% donkey
serum, 3% BSA, 1xPBS)","20 mL reconstituted donkey serum
180 mL 1X PBS
 6.0 g BSA
-----------------------
200 mL Total Volume

Mix thoroughly"],["DAPI Staining Solution","0.1 mL DAPI stock solution
499.9 mL 1X PBS
-----------------------
 500 mL Total Volume

Add 250 mL to 2 opaque staining dishes.
Discard after 10 uses. Working solution expires in 6 months from
preparation."],["Mounting Media","10 mL 1X PBS
 90 mL glycerol
 4.0 g Propyl Gallate
 1.0 g DABCO
-----------------------
100 mL Total Volume

Mix contents in a glass bottle and heat overnight in water bath at 60C. Keep protected from light. 

The next day make sure that all contents are in solution and filter with a 0.45uM filter. Cover with foil and store at 4C for up to 2 months. 

Alternate mounting media (50% glycerol, 4% propyl gallate) should be used with markers that leach."]],"guid":"E620C4002AE011EBA6A3CDE7C10C4B6E","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"7eg43","text":"Table 5: Summary of the required working solutions for the workflow.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}},"mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Working Solutions","Expiration"],["1X Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS)","100 mL 10x PBS

Shake stock unmasking solution vigorously prior to pipetting. Mix water and stock unmasking solution thoroughly in beaker. Make fresh prior to each antigen retrieval step. The pH should be 6.0"],["Tris Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS2)","If you’re using white coplin jars (50 mLs) -
 5 mL 10x Tris AR stock
45 mL ddH20
-----------------------
50 mL Total Volume

If you’re using green staining jars (hold ~250 mL)
 25 mL 10x Tris AR stock
 225 mL ddH20
-----------------------
 250 mL Total Volume

Mix water and stock Tris AR thoroughly in beaker.
Make fresh prior to each antigen retrieval step.
The pH should be 8.8-9.0"],["Slide Blocking Solution (10% donkey
serum, 3% BSA, 1xPBS)","20 mL reconstituted donkey serum
180 mL 1X PBS
 6.0 g BSA
-----------------------
200 mL Total Volume

Mix thoroughly"],["DAPI Staining Solution","0.1 mL DAPI stock solution
499.9 mL 1X PBS
-----------------------
 500 mL Total Volume

Add 250 mL to 2 opaque staining dishes.
Discard after 10 uses. Working solution expires in 6 months from
preparation."],["Mounting Media","10 mL 1X PBS
 90 mL glycerol
 4.0 g Propyl Gallate
 1.0 g DABCO
-----------------------
100 mL Total Volume

Mix contents in a glass bottle and heat overnight in water bath at 60C. Keep protected from light. 

The next day make sure that all contents are in solution and filter with a 0.45uM filter. Cover with foil and store at 4C for up to 2 months. 

Alternate mounting media (50% glycerol, 4% propyl gallate) should be used with markers that leach."]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}},"5":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"A_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"},"B_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"},"C_1":{"className":"_dt-h-center _dt-bold"},"C_2":{"className":"_dt-h-center"},"C_3":{"className":"_dt-h-center"},"C_4":{"className":"_dt-h-center"}},"colTitles":["","",""],"colWidths":["200","198.25","154"],"data":[["Working Solutions","Expiration","Storage"],["Citrate Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS1)","Daily: Make fresh prior to antigen retrieval step","N/A"],["Tris Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS2)","Daily: Make fresh prior to antigen retrieval step","N/A"],["Dapi","6 months from making","4 degrees"]],"guid":"866F38A02AE711EBA6A3CDE7C10C4B6E","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"9ouel","text":"Table 6: Summary of the required working solutions for the workflow.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}},"mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Working Solutions","Expiration","Storage"],["Citrate Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS1)","Daily: Make fresh prior to antigen retrieval step","N/A"],["Tris Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS2)","Daily: Make fresh prior to antigen retrieval step","N/A"],["Dapi","6 months from making","4 degrees"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}}}}
steps: This is the initial step of the deparaffinization process. This step serves two purposes: (1) melting of the paraffin wax and (2) ensuring tissue adherence to the slides.
Set the incubator to 60 °C .

Load tissue slides into a slide rack. All tissue slides should have the tissue side facing up and parallel to the rack. For fragile/fatty tissues, the slides may be placed in front of a fan overnight, before slide baking, to remove excess moisture.
Place slide rack (with tissue side facing up) into a 60 °C incubator.
Incubate for at least 01:00:00 . We recommend Overnight baking for optimal tissue retention.

After placing FFPE slides in the incubator for baking, prepare the following reagents – xylene, 100% ethanol, 95% ethanol, 70% ethanol, 50% ethanol, 1X PBS, and 1X PBS with 0.3% Triton X100. Reagent amounts ( 250 mL ) are for a staining dish that can contain up to 24 slides. If only 5 slides, use a coplin jar and make up all of the working solutions to a 50 mL final volume. Make sure to fill up the container only up to frost edge (lower edge). Avoid pouring solutions all the way up to the top of the slide (labels and pen writing on frosted edge may contaminate samples).
Before preparing reagents, place blue underpads on the bench to absorb spills.
After baking, follow steps below.
Transfer slides to xylene and wash, 00:05:00 each, with gentle agitation (1/2).
Place slides in 100% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (1/2).
Place slides in 95% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (1/2).
Place slides in 70% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (1/2).
Place slides in 50% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (1/2).
Place slides in PBS and wash for 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (1/2).
Permeabilize tissue in 1X PBS with 0.3% Triton X100 for 00:10:00 .

Wash in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 . During this wash, begin pre-warming the antigen retrieval solutions that are required for the next step. Refer to the "Antigen Retrieval" Section for additional information regarding solution prep.
During slide clearing and hydration prepare the reagents needed for antigen retrieval (ARS1+ARS2):
Prepare ARS1 (Citrate based solution):
Label a beaker or 250ml cylinder “ARS1”.
Add 237.5 mL, ddH20 to the beaker.
Remove Vector Labs citrate antigen retrieval solution from 4°C storage.
Shake citrate solution vigorously.
Add 12.5 mL, citrate solution to ARS1 beaker.
Add a magnetic stir bar, place on magnetic stirrer, and stir thoroughly (if using a graduated cylinder, cover with parafilm and invert to mix).
Transfer to a metal staining dish from the NxGen Decloaker labeled “ARS1.”
pH using Biocare pH strips, verify pH is pH 6.0 .
Prepare ARS2 (Tris based solution).
Label a beaker or 250ml cylinder “ARS2”.
Add 225 mL, ddH20 to the beaker.
Once the aliquot of 10x ARS2 has thawed, add it to the beaker. If the aliquot is partially thawed, you can pour the frozen solution into the water.
Add a magnetic stir bar, place on magnetic stirrer, and stir thoroughly. If using a graduated cylinder, cover with parafilm and invert to mix.
Transfer to a metal staining dish from the NxGen Decloaker labeled “ARS2.”
pH using Biocare pH strips, verify pH is between pH 8.5 and pH 9 .
Prepare the Biocare NxGen Decloaker.
Add 500 mL, DI water to the bottom of pressure cooker.
Be sure to add fresh DI water before each antigen retrieval run.

Fill 3 metal staining dishes (provided with the Biocare NxGen decloaker) containing the two antigen retrieval solutions (ARS1 and ARS2) and DI water ( 250 mL ). The maximum amount of slides per run is 24. pH - Citrate should be 6 and TRIS should be 8.5/9.
Place the staining dishes containing the antigen retrieval solutions in the wire rack in the outside positions and the staining dish containing the water in the center position.
Place the wire rack with the staining dishes into the water bath.
Plug the decloaker into a wall outlet to turn the unit on – it is not equipped with a power switch. The display screen will turn on.

Press the lower left hand button (Menu button: page with lines) once and a list of temperatures will appear. Select 110 °C using the menu button (lower left) and press the green circle (center button) once.
Set the time to 00:04:00 using the lower menu and timer buttons as indicated on the screen and press the green button to accept.
The pressure cooker is now waiting to start and can sit in this state until the slides are ready.

Complete the below checklist and include it in your documentation for the Antigen Retrieval run. A B C 1 Pre-Warming Antigen Retrieval Checklist Duration (min) Comments 2 Added 500mL of fresh DI water to thepressure cooker? 5 minutes 3 ARS1 and ARS2 prepared appropriately? Consult working solutions section. 4 pH’d both ARS1 and ARS2 prior to heating? ARS1 pH should be 6, ARS2 pH should be 8.5/9.

Place the lid on the Decloaking chamber and make sure it is in the locked position (metal button over the left handle); turn lid towards you to lock. Position the pressure limit valve as directed in image below, ensuring it is set level on the pressure stem, not tipped to one side.

Press the center green button once to begin the program. When the temperature display on the front of the decloaker shows 70 °C start an external timer for 00:20:00 . The slides will spend 20 minutes in ARS1, 20 minutes in ARS2, and then sit for 10 minutes on the benchtop in ARS2. The program will count down 4 minutes once the pressure cooker has reached the set temperature of 110°C. The external timer is used to time the 20 minutes required in ARS1 since the program does not do this.

Pressure cooker will hold at 110 °C for 00:04:00 . Once the 4 min hold is complete, record the temperature and pressure from the device display on the log sheet. Leave the slides in ARS1 solution for a total time of 00:20:00 (until the external timer beeps). Over the remaining time, the temperature should decrease.
After the external timer has beeped, record the displayed temperature and pressure on the log sheet.

Carefully open the lid (use gloves as the surface is hot). Water will run from the lid when tipped so be sure to not allow the water to pour into either of the AR solutions. Remove steam strip indicator, steam strip should be uniformly brown if passed (light brown is OK). Allow steam strip to dry and then tape into log sheet.

Transfer the slides into Tris-Antigen Retrieval working solution (ARS2) and close pressure cooker (it will not lock after the run is complete, but it will slowly cool down even though it is not in the locked position).

Restart the external timer for 00:20:00 . During the 20 minutes, the decloaker will beep to indicate the run has been completed. Press the green circle (center button) to stop the beeping, but leave the decloaker closed and slides in ARS2. The temperature will decrease during this time.
After the external timer has beeped, open pressure cooker, and record the final displayed temperature and pressure on the log sheet.
Check pH of solutions with test strips and record on log sheet. For the pH, AR solution 1 (citrate) should be pH 6 and AR solution 2 (Tris) should be pH 8.5 / pH 9 .
Remove the wire rack and place on the bench for 00:10:00 (keep slides in hot Tris-ARS2 during this cool-down).
Let the pressure cooker cool down to 50 °C . Unplug the pressure cooker to turn it off.
Wash slides ( 00:05:00 each) in PBS with gentle agitation on shaker (1/4).
Proceed to slide blocking.
During slide clearing and hydration prepare the reagents needed for slide blocking.
Remove two 10 mL, aliquots of reconstituted donkey serum from the -20°C freezer.
Allow the donkey serum to thaw at Room temperature .
Prepare Blocking Solution. Let the slides sit in 1X PBS until the blocking solution is ready.
Label a beaker “Blocking Solution.”
Add 180 mL, 1x PBS to the beaker.
Weigh out and add 6.0 g, Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) to the beaker.
Add a magnetic stir bar, place on magnetic stirrer, and stir thoroughly.
While stirring, add the 2x 10 mL, aliquots of donkey serum to the beaker.
Continue to mix thoroughly.
Cover with parafilm and leave at Room temperature until you are ready for slide blocking.
Once you’re ready for slide blocking, pour the prepared Slide Blocking Solution into a staining dish and transfer slides from 1X PBS into the Slide Blocking staining dish.
Incubate for at least 01:00:00 at Room temperature , or Overnight at 4 °C .
After the incubation, wash slides one time with fresh 1X PBS for 00:05:00 on an orbital shaker set to 60 RPM.
If DAPI working solution is not available, prepare it as per Table 5 in the "Reagent Prep" Section.
Pour DAPI solution into a staining dish.
Transfer blue slide rack (containing the slides) sitting in 1X PBS into the “DAPI” staining dish. FFPEs are stained with DAPI to label the cell nuclei for imaging purposes.
Incubate slides in DAPI working solution for 00:15:00 at Room temperature with gentle agitation on an orbital shaker set to 60 RPM.

After the 15 minute incubation, transfer the slide rack into a staining dish containing fresh 1X PBS. Do not discard the DAPI solution. The DAPI may be reused up to 10 times or by the expiration date, whichever comes first. Tally the number of uses on the DAPI bottle.
Wash slides 1x for 00:05:00 in fresh 1X PBS with gentle agitation on an orbital shaker set to 60 RPM.
Transfer slides to staining dish with dH 2 0 and proceed to coverslipping.
Mounting media should be removed from the 4°C fridge and brought to Room temperature .
Take a rainin pipet tip (200uL-green box) and with a scissors clip the end to make a wide bore.
Take up 50 µL, mounting media (4% propyl gallate, 1% DABCO, 90% glycerol) and add to one end of the slide. For leeching markers, use alternate mounting media (50% glycerol, 4% propyl gallate).
Place the end of the coverslip at a 45 degree angle and slowly lower; allowing the mounting media to flow under the coverslip across the slide.
Check to make sure there are no bubbles over the tissue. Also make sure there is not mounting media oozing out of the sides of the coverslip; remove any excess.
Proceed to Background Imaging or store the slides at 4 °C in light protected environment.
Remove a 25 mL aliquot of the 10x Tris Antigen Retrieval Solution (ARS2) from the -20°C freezer.
Allow the ARS2 to thaw at Room temperature .
Transfer slides to xylene and wash, 00:05:00 each, with gentle agitation (2/2).
Place slides in 100% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (2/2).
Place slides in 95% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (2/2).
Place slides in 70% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (2/2).
Place slides in 50% ethanol and wash, 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (2/2).
Place slides in PBS and wash for 00:05:00 each with gentle agitation (2/2).
Wash slides ( 00:05:00 each) in PBS with gentle agitation on shaker (2/4).
Wash slides ( 00:05:00 each) in PBS with gentle agitation on shaker (3/4).
Wash slides ( 00:05:00 each) in PBS with gentle agitation on shaker (4/4).

Place slides in the Citrate-Antigen Retrieval working solution (ARS1). Place steam test strip on the center staining (water) dish so that it rests across the top of the staining dish without touching liquid. Please note that the metal staining dishes do not have lids.
creator: Liz McDonough
